doc_title: IP 69009, "Class I Research and Test Reactor Fuel Movement."

steps.text: Be aware of the facility’s plans and schedules for refueling or other major fuel movement.

Review the safety analysis report (SAR) or as-built facility descriptions, the TS, the licensee’s procedure(s), and the Security Plan for fuel design information, fuel movement methods, and requirements.
During the onsite inspection, be alert for significant differences in the SAR, or the TS descriptions of fuel and equipment as compared to the actual fuel and equipment.
Determine whether the licensee’s fuel handling procedures are adequate to perform intended functions.
Determine whether fuel is moved and inspected consistent with the requirements of the TS and the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether fuel handling activities are conducted in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee’s procedures and programs for radiation protection.
Determine whether the licensee satisfied security plan requirements for fuel movement activities.
Determine whether significant fuel movement or inspection problems are identified and resolved in accordance with the licensee’s procedural controls.
Determine whether the licensee was within TS limits and met procedural requirements before resuming normal operation after fuel movement.

preconditions.text: If significant differences in the SAR or TS descriptions are identified, design change review and approval is required in accordance with 10 CFR 50.59 and the licensee’s procedures.

If direct observation of a fuel movement activity is not possible at the time of inspection, verify the final fuel location, review respective records, and conduct discussions with personnel involved.
If direct observation of radiological controls is not possible at the time of inspection, review radiation protection records and conduct discussions with responsible personnel.
If direct observation of security controls is not possible at the time of inspection, review records and conduct discussions with responsible personnel.

If direct observation of problem resolution is not possible at the time of inspection, identify problem areas by reviewing the licensee’s logs and records and conducting discussions with responsible personnel.
If direct observation of reactor startup is not possible at the time of inspection, review respective licensee logs and records and conduct discussions with responsible personnel.

constraints.text: Under no circumstances is the licensee to adjust schedules for these activities to fit the inspection schedule.
Reference to this guidance is to aid in the technical evaluation of licensee programs and is not to be used as requirements unless the licensee has committed to them in writing.
